caption:
We have been SOOOOOOO excited to announce this… 🤩🚨

We’re hosting an event with Timothy Armoo in London!!!

One of the most exciting voices in business right now - founder, investor, and someone who has built and scaled at an incredible level - @timarmoo will be joining us live for a special, intimate evening.

Inspired by his new book What’s Stopping You?, this is all about ambition, momentum, and breaking through to your next level.

And he’ll be interviewed live by @iamsumanrandhawa 👏

Back in November 2025, we hosted an evening with Daniel Priestley… and it was INCREDIBLE.

The energy, the conversations, the people in the room 👏

We genuinely cannot wait to do it all again with Timothy.

Expect:

💡 Honest, high-level insights

🎤 A live interview + your chance to ask Timo YOUR questions 

🤝 A room full of ambitious founders

🎁 Plus… a copy of Timo’s book What’s Stopping You? to take home

This is the kind of event where the conversations, ideas and connections can genuinely change what you do next.

📍 London, SE1
📅 21st May 2026
⏰ 6:30pm - 10:00pm

⚠️ Buuuuut, spaces are limited - first come, first served.

🎟 Go to the link in our bio for tickets, or comment TIMO and we’ll send you the ticket details!

#ATOMICON #TimothyArmoo #LondonEvents #EntrepreneurEvents #BusinessGrowth

caption:
Picture this: a student sitting on her sofa, overwhelmed and lonely, opens Instagram to document her journey into marketing. 

A week later, she's hit her first 1,000 followers. 

6 months later? 30,000 followers. 

4 years later? 600,000.

WOW.

Sophie Miller built a 600,000-strong community as a student during the pandemic, growing it to become one of the UK's most engaged marketing communities - all starting from her spare room.

Sophie is the founder of Pretty Little Marketer, with over 600,000 followers across social platforms and 600+ paying members. 

In this conversation, Sophie reveals:

- How she hit 1,000 followers in one week and 30,000 in 6 months without any strategy whatsoever 📈

- Why she waited 6 months before showing her face on the Instagram page

- The brutal truth about her first year running a membership (and why she almost shut it down completely) 🎯

- Why 75-80% of her revenue comes from brand partnerships, not her membership

- The content strategy that got Kim Kardashian to engage with her post (she didn't even tag her) 🚀

- Why she's posting LESS content this year (and how it's actually working better)

- How to create content faster by answering three simple questions about your business

- How she gained 210 members in one week after restructuring her membership model

Most creators think you need to monetise fast and post constantly to build a sustainable business.

Sophie proves you can build a huge community by serving first, that posting less is sometimes the best option, amongst a TON of other incredibly valuable takes.

This conversation goes deep into the reality of building solo - not hiring anyone for nearly 5 years, almost quitting when the membership wasn't working, and managing a life-changing diagnosis whilst having the biggest revenue year ever 👀

Go to our link in bio to listen, or comment the word “LINK” and we’ll send you the link to the ep!

#DontWorkHarder #ATOMIC #Marketing

caption:
Niall Ratcliffe and his brother Morgan grew up in Burnley council estates. 

They were living on £30 a month. 

Their goal was to make £100,000 a year and pay themselves £50,000 each.

They hit that goal in 11 days. 

Four years later, they're running a 7-figure agency 🚀

Niall is co-founder of Noticed, a B2B storytelling and marketing agency based in Manchester. 

He's grown his LinkedIn to 60,000 followers, built the agency from zero to 7 figures in under 4 years, worked with clients like NHS and Ocean Beach, and more. 

In this conversation, Niall reveals:

- How they hit £100K run rate in 11 days when potential clients started messaging them on LinkedIn 📈

- How they got through a 6-month plateau where the business just wasn't growing no matter what they tried

- The one perception shift that helped them work with enormous brands like NHS and Ocean Beach 🚀

- Why you're being lied to about inbound leads (They had 60K followers and even still inbound wasn't enough to fuel their growth)

- His EXACT cold outreach strategy that you can steal 

- How they dropped payroll by £15K/month in December, added 3 new clients in January, and became £25K/month more profitable

- How AI completely transformed the agency model (same work output with dramatically less people)

- The commitment ladder Daniel Priestley taught him at dinner in Belfast (changed their entire outreach approach)

This is a masterclass in scaling fast and breaking through growth plateaus. 

Niall breaks down the exact cold outreach strategy that took them to 7 figures in 4 years, how one perception shift helped them to sign huge enterprise clients, and why AI is changing the game for online businesses right now.

Whether you're building an agency, struggling with unpredictable inbound leads, or stuck at a revenue ceiling, this conversation shows the real strategy behind explosive growth 👀

Go to our link in bio to listen, or comment the word “LINK” and we’ll send you the link to the ep!

#DontWorkHarder

caption:
Jason Graystone’s YouTube video sat dormant. Then it exploded, making 1.5m million in 3 months from a £300 product shared at the end. And eventually that one video generated 12 million in revenue.

Jason spent 2 weeks mapping out a video so comprehensive that no one would need him after watching it. 

He gave everything away for free. 

And that's exactly why people paid.

Now he's financially free, runs a business he doesn't need, and is what he calls """"always free"""" 👀

Jason is a wealth enthusiast, trader, angel investor, and content creator with over 500,000 YouTube subscribers. 

He achieved financial freedom at through trading, built a business model so passive he doesn't resent showing up, and created an oversubscribed program with 10,000 people on the waiting list. 

In this conversation, Jason reveals:

- The YouTube video strategy that generated $12 million for his business 📈

- How he went from 50,000 to 390,000 subscribers in one year after 8 years of slow growth

- The 18 months he spent """"donating money to the markets"""" before the week that changed everything 👀

- The oversubscribed model he used to grow his business without ever placing an advert 

- Why he gives 30-minute Instagram stories with A-Z solutions and no call to action (and how that builds trust better than any sales strategy) 🤯

- The exercise his mentor made him do that changed everything (calculating the TRUE cost of what you want, not just the price tag)

- Why he has no goals, no aspirations - because he knew what contentment was before he got there 👀

Most entrepreneurs think financial freedom requires hustle, scale, and complexity.

Jason proves you can build indestructible wealth through diversification and giving away lots of value for free.

caption:
Last June I travelled over from Spain to Newcastle for ATOMICON 2025… and honestly, it was INCREDIBLE.

It was a weekend full of incredible conversations, big ideas and inspiring people. I met entrepreneurs from all over the world, learned from some amazing speakers and left with a notebook full of ideas.

Here were my 4 biggest takeaways:

🤝 You never regret starting a conversation.
Everyone was so friendly!! 

🎤 Talk to the speakers afterwards.

After the talks you can often meet the speakers, ask questions about your own business and get advice directly from people doing incredible things.

🧠 Don’t try to learn everything.

You’ll hear hundreds of ideas in one day. 

The real value comes from picking a few powerful takeaways and actually implementing them.

🔥 Being around ambitious entrepreneurs is seriously motivating.

When you’re surrounded by people excited about building and growing their businesses, it’s impossible not to feel inspired.

And the exciting part…

ATOMICON 2026 is coming.

📍 Newcastle upon Tyne
📅 June 16th 2026

And BIG NEWS… Phase 2 of the lineup is being revealed on Monday.

This is going to be epic 👀

caption:
What happens when you love what you do but can't sustain how hard you're working? 🙏

Frankie James built Ideas Forum into one of the UK's most respected entrepreneurship platforms: the Great British Entrepreneur Awards - with 5,500+ entries at its peak!

Plus... she built Ideasfest - the 4,000-person business festival that Steven Bartlett called ""Glastonbury for business."" 

Frankie has an incredible mindset when it comes to building a long term brand - she's turned down major sponsorship deals that didn't feel aligned with her values, and made the terrifying decision to cancel Ideasfest after selling 1,200 tickets - because she knew she couldn't deliver her best work. 

Her approach challenges big assumptions that the entrepreneurship space makes about hustle culture, operating with strong values, and enjoying your work.

In this value-packed conversation, Frankie reveals:

- The one question that made her CANCEL a sold-out event - and why sometimes stopping is braver than pushing through 👀

- Why loving what you do makes knowing your limits harder - and the warning signs she watches for now 🙏

- The powerful sales skill that she learned from going door-to-door, that has become her secret weapon for landing big partnerships 💰

- How she gets 5,500 people to enter her awards for FREE - and still builds a profitable business model 📈

- How she built a group of friends that TRULY understands and supports her through her struggles in business ❤️

And so much more. 

Frankie's built something massive with eight people because she's protected what matters for 12+ years - even when it meant walking away from money or pausing momentum.

For entrepreneurs who genuinely love their work but wonder how they can keep pushing without burning out... this episode shows what sustainable really looks like 🙌

Go to our link in bio to listen, or comment the word “LINK” and we’ll send you the link to the clip!

#DontWorkHarder #ATOMIC #Marketing

caption:
Think building powerful relationships requires years of experience, the perfect introduction, or being extroverted?

Simon Ong went from a shy, introverted kid to securing book endorsements from Marie Forleo, Simon Sinek, and Ali Abdal 👀

Now he's a global keynote speaker, Penguin-published author, and has built a business with multiple revenue streams 🚀 

Simon is a global keynote speaker, energy life coach, and the author of ""Energize."" 

He grew his YouTube channel from 0 to 130,000 subscribers in 10 months and has built strategic relationships by connecting with people outside his industry - film directors, chefs, musicians, not just other speakers. 

In this conversation, Simon reveals:

- How he built multiple revenue streams before the pandemic - and how he scaled his coaching income 📈

- How he designed his business to be extremely flexible so he can pivot and switch lanes fast when problems hit

- The voice note strategy on LinkedIn that converts nearly 100% of connections (and why it works better than text) 🎯

- The incredibly effective opening technique he uses for every keynote... inspired by James Bond 😎

- The huge mindset shift that he uses to take the pressure off networking and make it effortless 

- What he studied before posting his first YouTube video (and how it led to 130k subscribers in 10 months) 🚀

- Why his ""I'm 40 today"" video got 4 million views and gained him 76,000 subscribers from a single piece of content 🤯

Most entrepreneurs think networking is about collecting contacts and making asks when you need something.

Simon proves that relationship building is a daily habit, that the best opportunities come from years of adding value before ever asking, and tells us exactly what to do to make that sustainable.

For entrepreneurs who think they're ""not good at networking"" or believe powerful relationships are out of reach, this episode shows what becomes possible with the right mindset 👀

When I was 17, I started a content creation and content marketing business for online coaches - and during my first few months in business, I sent a cold DM to Andrew and Pete, to get them interested in my services 👀

3 years later in early 2025, I partnered up with the ATOMIC team to take on all of their content across ATOMICON, ATOMIC’s Instagram content, our show Don’t Work Harder, and more. 

And now - I’m going to be coming on here to share content around how to use content to grow your brand and get more clients.
 This has been an amazing journey so far, and I’m really grateful to be a part of the ATOMIC team.
 
Keep your eyes out for more content coming soon!

caption:
Fifth video call of the day. On her birthday. On a family holiday, watching her dad play with her daughter in the pool, whilst dealing with a crisis back at the office that only she could handle.

In that moment Amelia Sordell realised she'd built a prison, not a business - and spent the next 18 months deliberately ""unscaling"" the UK's leading personal branding agency to rebuild something aligned 💡

Now she's building Mother - a media company for women, by women - and teaching female founders to use their voice to gain competitive advantage in a space dominated by men.

Amelia is a personal branding consultant, best-selling author, and keynote speaker with over 250,000 LinkedIn followers and 70,000 on Instagram. She scaled Klowt to £4 million revenue with high overheads, generated billions of organic views for clients, then walked away because she couldn't scale a business built entirely around her personal brand. 

In this conversation, Amelia reveals:

- Why she spent 18 months ""unscaling"" a successful agency 👀

- How building a brand exclusively around your expertise makes scaling really trick

- Why 80% of young people can't name a single female founder 

- How she monetises social media with a highly profitable system (£79 course as paid lead magnet, 1,000 sales, then consulting at £5.5K/day) 🚀

- Why you need to separate your personality from your content 

Most personal branding experts teach you to scale your influence.

This conversation goes deep into the reality of burnout - living in stress, having nothing left to give at home, being a stressed parent, and the gradual process of dismantling something you spent 5 years building - for the greater good.

Go to our link in bio to listen, or comment the word “LINK” and we’ll send you the link to the ep!

#DontWorkHarder #ATOMIC #Marketing
